ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 (OceanLotus / SeaLotus / Canvas Cyclone) Outlook persistence chaining to Squiblydoo / mshta script-proxy execution
ucs.description: Hunts the APT32-characteristic chain where Office Application Startup persistence (malicious Outlook form / VbaProject.OTM) causes outlook.exe to spawn a signed-binary script proxy (regsvr32 /i: scrobj.dll, mshta http(s)://, or encoded PowerShell). FireEye and ESET have repeatedly tied this exact parent->child handoff to OceanLotus's KerrDown / Cobalt Kitty loader chains.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE Processes.parent_process_name="outlook.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND NOT match(Processes.user,"(?i)(\$$|^SYSTEM$|^LOCAL SERVICE$|^NETWORK SERVICE$)") BY Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval squiblydoo=if(match(process,"(?i)regsvr32.*/[ius]:.*(scrobj|https?://)"),1,0) | eval mshta_remote=if(match(process,"(?i)mshta.*https?://"),1,0) | eval encoded_ps=if(match(process,"(?i)(-enc(odedcommand)?\s|-ec\s|frombase64string|downloadstring|invoke-expression|\biex\b)"),1,0) | eval rundll32_remote=if(match(process,"(?i)rundll32.*(https?://|\\\\)"),1,0) | where squiblydoo=1 OR mshta_remote=1 OR encoded_ps=1 OR rundll32_remote=1 OR process_name IN ("regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "outlook.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| extend SquiblyDoo  = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)regsvr32.*/[ius]:.*(scrobj|https?://)"
| extend MshtaRemote = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)mshta.*https?://"
| extend EncodedPS   = ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ","FromBase64String","DownloadString","Invoke-Expression","IEX")
| extend Rundll32Remote = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)rundll32.*(https?://|\\\\)"
| where SquiblyDoo or MshtaRemote or EncodedPS or Rundll32Remote
     or FileName in~ ("regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe")   // bare LOLBin spawn from outlook.exe is itself anomalous
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SquiblyDoo, MshtaRemote, EncodedPS, Rundll32Remote, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                  // requires command-line auditing GPO
| where tolower(ParentProcessName) endswith @"\outlook.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\powershell.exe"
    or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\pwsh.exe"
    or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\cmd.exe"
    or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\mshta.exe"
    or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\regsvr32.exe"
    or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\rundll32.exe"
    or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\wscript.exe"
    or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\cscript.exe"
| extend SquiblyDoo  = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)regsvr32.*/[ius]:.*(scrobj|https?://)"
| extend MshtaRemote = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)mshta.*https?://"
| extend EncodedPS   = CommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ","FromBase64String","DownloadString","Invoke-Expression","IEX")
| extend Rundll32Remote = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)rundll32.*(https?://|\\\\)"
| where SquiblyDoo or MshtaRemote or EncodedPS or Rundll32Remote
     or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\regsvr32.exe"
     or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith @"\mshta.exe"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, SquiblyDoo, MshtaRemote, EncodedPS, Rundll32Remote
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 Outlook Persistence Spawning Script Proxy / LOLBin
id: 9f6b2a40-0de8-4f9c-a3e2-12c8f6ff77ab
status: experimental
description: Detects outlook.exe spawning a script interpreter or LOLBin (regsvr32 /i: scrobj.dll, mshta http(s)://, encoded powershell), the canonical APT32 (OceanLotus / SeaLotus / Canvas Cyclone) Office Application Startup persistence chain leading into KerrDown / Cobalt Kitty loaders.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2017/05/cyber-espionage-apt32.html
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2018/03/13/oceanlotus-ships-new-backdoor/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1137
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0050
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\outlook.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_intent_squibly:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)regsvr32.*/[ius]:.*(scrobj|https?://)'
  selection_intent_mshta:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)mshta.*https?://'
  selection_intent_ps:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-enc '
      - '-ec '
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'IEX'
  selection_intent_lolbin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and (1 of selection_intent_*) and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Outlook add-ins or VBA macros invoking PowerShell / scripts
  - Custom mail-rule automation tooling
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT32 is one of the few state-sponsored groups that has consistently used Office Application Startup (Outlook.OTM / Outlook custom forms) for persistence (FireEye 2017, ESET 2018, Volexity 2017), and they pair it with regsvr32 /i: scrobj.dll Squiblydoo and mshta-driven loaders for KerrDown / Cobalt Kitty. The narrow parent=outlook.exe + child=script-proxy + APT32-style intent regex eliminates the

_field_issues.message: `MshtaRemote` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `MshtaRemote` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

_sigma_issues:
yaml/sigma error: mapping values are not allowed in this context
  in "<unicode string>", line 4, column 86

ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 DLL search-order hijack: signed third-party utility side-loading from user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts the OceanLotus / SeaLotus signature side-loading pattern: a legitimately-signed Symantec / McAfee / Avast / Kaspersky / Adobe / Microsoft binary copied into %APPDATA% / %PROGRAMDATA% / %TEMP% loads an attacker-supplied DLL from the same folder. APT32 has reused this primitive for years (RasTls.exe + rastlsc.dll, wsc_proxy.exe + wsc.dll, McUtil.dll, AcroBroker abuse).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\Temp\\*")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("rastls.exe","rastlsc.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","wsc_proxy_x86.exe","goopdate.exe","AcroBroker.exe","RtkAudUService64.exe","TrendUploader.exe","Qcheck.exe","oleview.exe") OR (Processes.process_company IN ("Symantec Corporation","McAfee, Inc.","AVAST Software","Kaspersky Lab","Microsoft Corporation","Adobe Inc.","Sophos Limited","Trend Micro Inc.") AND Processes.process_signature_verified="true")) BY Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.process_hash Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=false count FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem WHERE Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Temp\\*")) BY Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_path as dll_path file_name as dll_name file_hash as dll_hash] | eval exe_dir=replace(process_path,"\\[^\\]+$","") | eval dll_dir=replace(dll_path,"\\[^\\]+$","") | where lower(exe_dir)==lower(dll_dir) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT32 (OceanLotus) DLL side-loading: signed-vendor EXE in user-writable path loads sibling DLL
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\"]);
let _abused_signers = dynamic(["Symantec","McAfee","AVAST","Avast","Kaspersky","Microsoft Corporation","Adobe","Sophos","Trend Micro"]);
let _ocean_lotus_loaders = dynamic(["rastls.exe","rastlsc.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","wsc_proxy_x86.exe","goopdate.exe","acrobroker.exe","rtkaudUservice64.exe","trenduploader.exe","qcheck.exe","oleview.exe"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_abused_signers)
     or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_ocean_lotus_loaders)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256                       // not the EXE re-mapping itself
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
     and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
// Co-located DLL = classic search-order hijack
| where tolower(FolderPath) =~ tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          SignedExe = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          SignedExePath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          SignedExeCompany = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
          SignedExeSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          LoadedDll = FileName, LoadedDllPath = FolderPath,
          LoadedDllSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sysmon EID 7 image-load via AMA / Windows Event collector
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\"]);
let _abused_signers = dynamic(["Symantec","McAfee","AVAST","Avast","Kaspersky","Microsoft Corporation","Adobe","Sophos","Trend Micro"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         CompanyName = tostring(EventData.Company),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         User        = tostring(EventData.User),
         ImageSha256 = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| where Image has_any (_user_writable)
| where not(Image startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where CompanyName has_any (_abused_signers)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\")
     and not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| extend ImageDir       = extract(@"^(.*\\)[^\\]+$", 1, Image),
         ImageLoadedDir = extract(@"^(.*\\)[^\\]+$", 1, ImageLoaded)
| where tolower(ImageDir) == tolower(ImageLoadedDir)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, CompanyName, Signed,
          ImageLoaded, ImageSha256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 (OceanLotus) DLL Side-Loading via Signed Utility in User-Writable Path
id: c1d2e8b3-3acb-4f19-9b8b-66a09d57e3cf
status: experimental
description: Detects DLL side-loading where a signed Symantec / McAfee / Avast / Kaspersky / Adobe / Microsoft / Sophos / Trend Micro utility runs from a user-writable directory (AppData / ProgramData / Public / Temp) and loads a co-located DLL. This is APT32's (OceanLotus / SeaLotus / Canvas Cyclone) signature execution primitive across RasTls.exe, wsc_proxy.exe, McUtil.dll, AcroBroker, and goopdate.exe campaigns.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2017/05/cyber-espionage-apt32.html
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2018/03/13/oceanlotus-ships-new-backdoor/
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0050
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Temp\'
  selection_signed_company:
    Company|contains:
      - 'Symantec'
      - 'McAfee'
      - 'AVAST'
      - 'Avast'
      - 'Kaspersky'
      - 'Microsoft Corporation'
      - 'Adobe'
      - 'Sophos'
      - 'Trend Micro'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
  filter_system_dll:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  filter_legit_install:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: selection_image_path and selection_signed_company and selection_dll and not filter_system_dll and not filter_legit_install
falsepositives:
  - Portable / extracted vendor utilities run from user profile (uncommon in managed estates)
  - Vendor uninstaller staged to %TEMP% during patching
level: high
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading from a signed-vendor binary running outside Program Files is the single most consistent APT32 / OceanLotus tradecraft fingerprint across a decade of public reporting (FireEye, ESET, Cybereason 'Operation Cobalt Kitty', Volexity). The combination of (1) abused vendor (Symantec/McAfee/etc.), (2) execution from user-writable path, and (3) co-located unsigned DLL load is rare for legi

_field_issues.message: `InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents.
